reviewer: build-wave agent (real headless run + responsive matrix, revision 1)

rubric.evidence: REAL browser run (own headless Chrome): loaded onnx-community/FastVLM-0.5B-ONNX via AutoModelForImageTextToText on the WASM CPU path (all-q4) and generated a grounded caption for the huggingface bee.jpg — 'The image depicts a vibrant scene in nature featuring a large, pink flower with a prominent central petal…' (GEN_MS≈41000 on shared software CPU) — a real, image-specific description, not boilerplate. This is the exact worker code path the page uses (AutoModelForImageTextToText + apply_chat_template + processor(image,prompt) + generate/TextStreamer). The demo page was also driven headless (first-visit Download honoured, Ready on WASM). conformance: loads-clean, calls-real-inference.
rubric.notes: llava_qwen2 (LlavaQwen2ForCausalLM) is registered in transformers.js 3.7.5 — no version-pin needed. Deliberately WASM-only: the WebGPU exports are unreliable today (fp16/q4f16 throw an ORT-Web `Unexpected input data type` because the fp16 vision encoder wants fp16 pixel_values but the processor emits float32; all-q4 on a *software* WebGPU adapter returned degenerate tokens — both measured in headless Chrome). CPU keeps the output correct and identical on every device.

rubric.evidence: Output is streamed text tokens conditioned on the image (image-text-to-text): the caption content tracks the input image (pink flower for bee.jpg). Token stream + constructed-prompt surface expose the exact chat-templated prompt and per-token arrival times.

conformance: declares-model-id=pass (onnx-community/FastVLM-0.5B-ONNX linked), declares-task=pass (image-text-to-text), declares-backend=pass (WASM), declares-quantisation=pass (q4). The readout reports the actual device used (WASM) after each run.
rubric.notes: Dtype honestly disclosed: all-q4 (embed/vision/decoder) on the WASM CPU backend. The fp16/q4f16 variants throw an ORT-Web 'Unexpected input data type' error (FastVLM's fp16 vision encoder wants fp16 pixel_values but the processor emits float32 and Transformers.js doesn't cast); all-q4 on a software WebGPU adapter is degenerate — both measured in headless Chrome. ~1.4 GB download (the q4 embedding table is large).

rubric.evidence: shared-auto-init-loader=pass. First visit surfaced the Download (~1.4 GB) button — never a silent large download (invariant 12) — and the model loaded after the click. Returning-user auto-init + clear-cache handled by the shared createModelLoader.

conformance: live-status-region=pass, errors-on-the-page=pass. Generation failures surface a labelled .err status; the readout reports the real backend (WASM). No requiresWebGPU gate — the model runs on the CPU, so it works on any device (no GPU needed).
rubric.notes: WebGPU intentionally not used (unreliable exports today).

rubric.evidence: Every control operable: sample thumbnails (click + Enter/Space, role=button), drag/drop upload, prompt input, quick-prompt chips, max-tokens number input, Answer button. buttons-have-names=pass. Verified via the real run.
The answer is streamed straight from model.generate via a TextStreamer; confirmed by the grounded bee.jpg caption that varies with the image. No canned result.

conformance: images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass. Status is role=status aria-live; the answer + token stream are aria-live; sample thumbnails and dropzone are keyboard-operable (Enter/Space). Applied modern-web-guidance 'accessibility'.

responsive-check: desktop clean · mobile clean (no horizontal overflow, no console/network errors, both classes). The vlm-grid uses flex 1 1 <basis> columns that stack on mobile; images use max-inline-size:100%. Applied 'css-layout'.

Generation runs off the main thread in a Web Worker, streaming tokens; the control UI stays responsive. On WASM CPU the model is slow (~41 s for 50 tokens on a shared software runner; faster on real user CPUs) but honest — the readout reports real latency + tok/s. conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass.
rubric.notes: A hardware-GPU WebGPU path would be faster but is blocked by unreliable exports today; recorded as an open question.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible streaming output status live region keyboard operable image picker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce load/progress/ready/error in a live region; label every control; keyboard-operate custom controls; stream into an aria-live region.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live status, aria-live answer + token stream, labelled buttons/inputs, Enter/Space handlers on sample thumbnails + dropzone.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive image + output two-column layout without horizontal overflow mobile desktop
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Flex columns with flex:1 1 <basis>, max-inline-size on media, logical properties; wrap on narrow viewports.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — .vlm-grid flex-wrap with flex:1 1 280px/300px columns, preview img max-inline-size:100%, logical properties throughout.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep UI responsive INP stream tokens off main thread
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run inference off the main thread and stream progress/tokens incrementally to keep INP low.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — generation runs in a Web Worker; tokens stream to the UI via postMessage, so the main thread never blocks on generation.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (shared design system).

openQuestions: FastVLM is licensed apple-amlr (research / non-commercial) — the page states this in at-a-glance; is a clearer license banner warranted before the Run-it panel?

WebGPU would be much faster but the current onnx-community exports are unreliable in Transformers.js (fp16/q4f16 throw an input-dtype error; all-q4 is degenerate on a software WebGPU adapter). Re-check on an updated export / real-GPU runner to offer an optional WebGPU fast path.
On low-memory mobile the ~1.4 GB download + WASM decode may be too heavy; should the page detect memory and suggest a smaller clip / warn proactively?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a webcam 'point-and-ask' wild demo (user-initiated capture) that sends a single frame to FastVLM.

summary: Revision-1 build-wave pass for fastvlm-vlm (image-text-to-text, onnx-community/FastVLM-0.5B-ONNX, WASM all-q4). Real in-browser inference confirmed (bee.jpg -> grounded pink-flower caption, the exact page worker path); first-visit Download honoured; desktop+mobile responsive clean; console clean. Deliberately WASM-only after measuring that the WebGPU exports are unreliable today (fp16/q4f16 input-dtype error; all-q4 degenerate on a software WebGPU adapter) — CPU keeps output correct on every device. A genuinely NEW VLM family (Apple FastViTHD + Qwen2-0.5B, llava_qwen2) distinct from the built SmolVLM/Moondream/Florence/PaliGemma/Qwen2-VL/nanoLLaVA. Support seeded needs-review pending a full agent screenshot matrix.
